reflection:
Yesterday the data tells a story you might not be seeing. On the 13th you had 12,318 steps and 105 minutes of daylight. On the 14th you had 8,044 steps and 9 minutes of daylight. Nine minutes. Recovery fell 17 points overnight, from 84 to 67. Then a light ping pong session with Lara left your body more wiped than you've ever felt. That sequence makes sense actually. Less light, less movement, less anchoring, and a nervous system already running hot.

Reading your sleep notes, you wrote about new wrist pain, sitting bones flaring, tinnitus louder, ice that no longer helps, head pressure. That combination plus body exhaustion after light effort reads like central sensitization having a flare day. Not a virus invading. Your PCR was negative.

The harder thing to say. You spent the afternoon deep in a Lyme rabbit hole and cancelled the sleep specialist because you told her you were sick. That is the avoidance pattern. The sleep work is what actually moves the needle. Lyme research is useful but it is not treatment. Don't let one fund the cancellation of the other.

Today Synevo draws the tick-borne panel. Get the test done, then close the laptop and go outside for 30 minutes. Sunlight on skin. Turkey is two weeks out. You are going. Be okay.
opener: 9 minutes of daylight yesterday. that is the story, not the Lyme rabbit hole.
focus_for_today: do the synevo draw, then 30 min outside with the laptop closed
